title: Best Available Techniques (BAT) Reference Document for the Food, Drink and Milk Industries (FDM BREF)
licence: EU Commission reuse policy (free with attribution)
notes: PRIORITY: canonical EU IPPC document. Direct PDF fetch blocked by tool provenance constraints; full ~700pp PDF must be downloaded manually from EIPPCB. URL recorded for engine RAG stub.

title: AHDB Dairy Energy Efficiency Resources
licence: AHDB free-to-read content; reuse beyond fair use requires AHDB permission
notes: Knowledge library spread across many pages; URLs catalogued in file. AHDB primary focus is dairy farms not processing plants.

title: BBPA Brewing Green and Road to Net Zero — UK Brewery Energy Benchmarks
licence: BBPA public web pages; member-only data behind login
notes: Public summary numbers captured. Full BBPA biennial member energy survey is member-only; record manual_required for plant-level data.

title: BSDA — UK Soft Drinks Sector Energy and Sustainability References
notes: BSDA does not publish a dedicated sector-wide energy report. Plant-level data requires BSDA membership.

title: DESNZ / BEIS Energy Consumption in the UK (ECUK) and MSEES
publisher: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ)
notes: ECUK is the UK's authoritative national energy statistics. MSEES 2021 covers manufacturing energy efficiency including F&D.

title: WRAP — UK Food and Drink Decarbonisation and Resource Efficiency
notes: Phase 2 'Unlocking Resource Efficiency' (Defra/WRAP April 2024) is the deepest recent open analysis of UK F&D energy and resource efficiency.

title: Carbon Trust — Refrigeration Systems Technology Guide (CTG046)
licence: Carbon Trust free guides with attribution
notes: Canonical UK introductory guide for industrial refrigeration energy savings. Sister guides CTG021 (roadmap) and CTL056 (heat recovery) catalogued.

title: DECC/BIS Industrial Decarbonisation and Energy Efficiency Roadmap to 2050 — Food and Drink Sector
publisher: DECC / BIS — Parsons Brinckerhoff and DNV GL
notes: PRIORITY: canonical UK Government commissioned consultancy study — closest existing analogue to the AI engine output. 2017 Action Plan also catalogued.

title: UK Industrial Decarbonisation Strategy (BEIS, March 2021, CP 399)
licence: Open Government Licence v3.0 / Crown Copyright CP 399
notes: Foundational UK industrial decarbonisation policy. Sets the strategic backdrop for all UK industrial decarb work post-2021.

title: Ricardo-AEA — Decarbonisation of Heat in Industry (Literature Review)
notes: Foundational UK industrial heat decarb evidence base. Pairs with Ricardo Pathways to Industrial Heat Decarbonisation white paper.

title: Element Energy — Deep-Decarbonisation Pathways for UK Industry (CCC, 2020)
publisher: Element Energy (now ERM) for the Climate Change Committee
licence: CCC public, free to download with attribution
notes: Introduces N-ZIP model. Underpinned CCC Sixth Carbon Budget industrial pathways.

title: FoodDrinkEurope — Decarbonisation Roadmap for the European F&D Manufacturing Sector
publisher: FoodDrinkEurope; analysis by Ricardo Energy & Environment
notes: ~90 named decarbonisation measures structured by demand and supply. Ricardo-authored, UK-credible.

title: AtkinsRéalis (Atkins / SNC-Lavalin) — Industrial Decarbonisation Roadmaps Programme
licence: AtkinsRéalis public press releases, citation only
notes: BEIS-appointed Engineering Delivery Partner for Industry of Future programme. Underlying client deliverables confidential.

title: WSP — 2050 Industrial Decarbonisation Roadmaps (with DNV-GL, for BEIS/DECC)
licence: Open Government Licence v3.0 (GOV.UK reports); WSP page © WSP
notes: WSP led Phase 2 Action Plans across 8 sectors including F&D. Same body of work as DECC 2015/2017 documents.

title: Mott MacDonald — Carbon Crunch and Path to Zero Carbon Heat
licence: Mott MacDonald public web content, citation only
notes: Useful as scene-setting / market context. No dedicated public F&D sector report. Heat decarb three-pathway framing widely cited.

title: Frazer-Nash Consultancy (KBR) — Industrial Decarbonisation References
licence: Frazer-Nash public web content, citation only
notes: Most FN decarb output client-confidential. Public material limited to press releases. Target buyer for engine seat-licence.

title: FDF — Roadmap to Net Zero Overview / Decarbonisation of Heat across UK F&D Manufacturing
notes: FDF is the principal UK F&D manufacturing trade body. Net Zero Handbook is member-facing guidance.

title: AEA Technology / Ricardo-AEA — UK Industrial Sector Decarbonisation Pathways (Pre-2015 Evidence Base)
publisher: AEA Technology / Ricardo-AEA for DECC and BEIS
notes: Cross-reference under AEA brand to the Ricardo-AEA evidence base. AEA was acquired by Ricardo in 2012.
